items: Baby wakes — greet them warmly, make eye contact before picking up
Diaper change — narrate every step, let baby kick and move freely
Get dressed — name clothes, offer choices if possible, let baby "help"
Feed — sit together, name the food, let baby self-feed what they can
Tummy time — a few minutes on the floor after the feed settles
Floor play — toys within reach, you nearby, baby chooses what to explore
sections.what_is_built: Language (narration), Motor (tummy time, grasping), Independence (self-feeding, choices), Routine (same sequence daily)

items: Feed — same routine: bib on, sit down, eat, hands wiped, bib off, done
Outside time — feel the air, name what you see and hear, touch natural things
Floor play — different toys or different room, baby leads the exploration
Nap — consistent place, consistent wind-down, baby's body learns the rhythm
Wake and diaper — narrate it, give baby a moment to stretch and move
sections.what_is_built: Sensory (outdoor exploration), Numeracy (counting, spatial words during play), Social-Emotional (security through consistency), Routine (predictable midday flow)

items: Feed — dinner together at the table when possible, even if baby is small
Bath time — warm water, sensory play, name body parts and sensations
Pajamas — let baby participate in dressing, name what you are doing
Read-aloud — one board book minimum, baby holds and touches it
Song — same song every night, the final signal before sleep
Bed — same closing words, same place, lights off
sections.what_is_built: Routine (the bedtime sequence is the most important routine you will ever build), Language (read-aloud, narration), Social-Emotional (the safety of consistent endings)

weekly_checkin: What is my baby doing now that they were not doing last week?
Which part of our rhythm is strongest?
What did I enjoy most this week?

items: Wake up. Check the weather together from the window.
Diaper change or potty (if ready). Child pulls pants up/down.
Get dressed. Child does as much as they can. Two choices, they pick.
Breakfast at the table. Child carries plate. Uses utensil. Pours from small pitcher.
Child carries plate to counter when done. Wipes face with cloth.
Morning outside time — long if possible. Walk, run, explore, collect, observe.
Come inside. Shoes by the door. Wash hands.
Lunch at the table. Name the food. Count the pieces. Practice "please" and "thank you."
Cleanup: child helps wipe table, carries plate.
Diaper change or potty.
Play time — child chooses the activity. Cleanup before switching.
Read-aloud or book time (quiet, seated, 10-15 minutes).
Nap or rest time (consistent location, consistent routine).
Wake from nap. Snack at the table.
Afternoon outside time or active indoor play.
Help with one real household task: watering plants, wiping surfaces, sorting laundry, sweeping.
Dinner at the table with family. Conversation. Name emotions from the day.
Bath time. Child undresses, climbs in, washes self (you finish).
Pajamas. Child puts them on (you help with hard parts).
Brush teeth. Child brushes first, you finish.
One book. Read-aloud together.
Bed. Same words every night. Lights out.
**Wake up** — child gets up, uses the bathroom, brushes teeth independently
**Get dressed** — child checks weather, selects clothes, dresses fully (help only with laces or difficult fasteners)
**Make bed** — every morning, without exception. Not perfect — done
**Breakfast** — child helps prepare or prepares own simple meal, pours own drink, sits at the table
**Clear place** — plate to sink, wipe spot, push in chair
**Chore** — the child's owned chore, done without being asked
**Morning read-aloud** — 10-15 minutes, followed by narration ("Tell me what happened")
sections.what_is_built: Self-directed routine (IC1.1-A1 Part 1), chore ownership (IC1.1-A1 Part 2), task completion (IC1.1-A1 Part 2), narration practice

items: **Outside time** — one hour minimum. Nature observation, gross motor activity, or nature journaling
**Free play / activities** — child-directed. Cleanup between activities — no reminders if possible
**Practical life** — real contribution: kitchen helping, gardening, laundry, sweeping, animal care
**Lunch** — child helps prepare, serves self, clears place independently
**Quiet time / rest** — 30-60 minutes. Books, drawing, or rest. Not screens. The capacity to be quiet and occupied without a screen is a Phase 1 readiness indicator
**Second read-aloud** — followed by narration. "Tell it back to me in your own words."
sections.what_is_built: Sustained outdoor engagement, independence through practical life, cleanup-before-transition (IC1.1-A1 Part 3), narration skill

items: **Afternoon activity** — play, errands, or outdoor time
**Dinner preparation** — child participates in real cooking tasks
**Dinner** — family table. Conversation. Child describes their day to someone who was not present for all of it
**Cleanup** — child clears own place and helps with one dinner cleanup task
**Bath** — child manages independently (parent present for safety)
**Bedtime read-aloud** — third read-aloud of the day, followed by narration
**Daily review** — "What went well today? What was hard? What will you do better tomorrow?"
**Bedtime routine** — consistent, predictable, non-negotiable. Same sequence every night
sections.what_is_built: Narration to absent audience (key Phase 1 skill), reflective self-assessment (IC1.1-A1 Part 5), full self-care independence, bedtime routine as anchor
